channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dangerous obstruction to Public foot path, 27th March
item.description:
As a matter of urgency Please can you have this removed as I hurt myself on the scaffold poles whilst walking through this footpath , there are no signs up pre warning you about the danger ahead when you enter this public right of way , thank you .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cleveleys Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working Lansdowne Gardens SW8 2EG, 27th March
item.description:
Very dark/dangerous due to street light not working.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lansdowne Gardens, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large oil spillage, 27th March
item.description:
The is a large oil spillage outside number 58 overton and it is running toward the drain outside number 60, looks like someone has down an oil change but let it drain into the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Overton Road, West Heath, Greater London

item.title: White tarps on dual carriageway. Not sure exactly where, 27th March
item.description:
Saw it abt an hour ago
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wickham Lane, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard, 27th March
item.description:
This report is to formally notify the Council of a significant pedestrian safety hazard located in the immediate vicinity of the main entrance to Bannockburn Surgery. The structural integrity of the pavement has deteriorated, creating an unacceptable risk of injury to our patients and the general public. 2. Location and Description Location: Along 20-22 Benares Road Hazard Description: The pavement surface is severely cracked, uneven, and contains sunken paving slabs. In several sections, the vertical displacement between slabs exceeds the safety threshold for "tripping hazards" as defined by standard highway inspection criteria. 3. Risk Assessment & Vulnerable Demographics As a primary care medical facility, our site experiences high footfall from specific demographics who are at an elevated risk of falls: Elderly Patients: Many of our patients possess frailty markers and reduced peripheral vision, making uneven surfaces difficult to navigate safely. Mobility-Restricted Individuals: Patients using wheelchairs, walking frames, or crutches are finding the entrance inaccessible and dangerous. Parents with Strollers: The uneven ground is causing difficulty for those entering the surgery with young children. A fall in this specific location could lead to severe physical injury, potentially resulting in hospital admission for already vulnerable individuals. 4. Required Action Given the proximity to a healthcare setting, we request that the Council: Conduct an urgent site inspection within the next 48 hours. Apply temporary safety markings (high-visibility paint) to the edges of the hazard immediately. Schedule a permanent repair as a priority "Category 1" defect to ensure the safety and accessibility of the surgery entrance.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bannockburn Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light trespass/pollution due to new led lightbulb, 27th March
item.description:
Two days ago, a new led lightbulb was installed in the street light post creating a severe light trespass and light pollution.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Finborough Road, Brompton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van has been abandoned outside our house for weeks., 27th March
item.description:
Car has not moved. Makes it difficult for visitors to park outside our house when required and also blocks view pulling off the drive way. Checked and doesn’t belong to any neighbours.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cloisters Avenue, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
